g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I register a GitHub webhook for my repo?')
sendPrompt('What does the webhook receiver endpoint do?')
sendPrompt('What is the job queue used for in the webhook system?')
sendPrompt('What does the diff worker do step by step?')
sendPrompt('What does the AI-Engine partial scan do?')
sendPrompt('How does Neo4j get patched with new graph data?')
sendPrompt('How does the SSE broadcast work to update the frontend?')
sendPrompt('What data is stored in the webhook_subscriptions table?')
sendPrompt('How does Neo4j store the new graph after a patch?')
sendPrompt('How does the React frontend receive live graph updates?')
g.text: EventSource('/api/events') — live graph patch via SSE
Webhooks augment the existing scan flow — they do not replace it
